deep_dive.title: Randomized neural operator for parametric PDEs with fast training and conformal uncertainty quantification
deep_dive.abstract: Repeatedly solving parametric PDEs is essential for uncertainty quantification, design optimization and inverse problems, but conventional neural operators require expensive non-convex training. We introduce PCA--RaNN, a randomized latent neural operator that combines PCA-based dimensionality reduction with fixed random features and a closed-form least-squares readout. It recasts latent operator learning as fixed-feature linear regression, reducing training time by one to three orders of magnitude across benchmarks while maintaining competitive accuracy. We introduce an energy-matched scaling rule and a lightweight two-parameter BFGS refinement to correct suboptimal feature scales. Ensemble averaging reduces predictive variance. On Burgers, Darcy, Navier--Stokes and backward heat equation benchmarks, PCA--RaNN provides a favorable speed--accuracy trade-off against operator-learning baselines. The ensemble supports split-conformal prediction intervals, and the linear readout enables rapid online adaptation via recursive least squares without retraining hidden features. This provides an efficient, uncertainty-aware surrogate for many-query scientific workflows.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: neural operator for parametric PDEs with fast training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于参数化PDE的神经算子，训练快速
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于参数化PDE的神经算子，训练快速
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于参数化PDE的神经算子，训练快速
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PCA-RaNN, a randomized latent neural operator combining PCA and random features with closed-form least-squares, achieving one to three orders of magnitude speedup for parametric PDEs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对参数化偏微分方程反复求解效率低的问题，提出PCA-RaNN随机潜在神经算子。该方法结合PCA降维与随机固定特征，通过闭式最小二乘训练，将训练时间降低1-3个数量级，并保持竞争精度。可高效代理化学传输与空气质量模型。

quick_skim.title: Evaluation of medium range machine learning models for sub-seasonal prediction
quick_skim.abstract: The performance of two machine learning (ML) atmosphere models - GraphCast and FourCastNetV2 - is evaluated in the context of sub-seasonal prediction, including their ability to represent key climate drivers of variability, namely the Madden-Julian Oscillation and the Southern Annular Mode. Model skill is assessed over both a 38-year hindcast period and a 2.5-year hindcast period. The longer period overlaps with the training windows of the ML models but provides a larger sample for robust evaluation, while the shorter period is independent of the ML model training period. This dual evaluation illustrates a compromise approach to the problem of insufficient independent data for evaluation of the models for sub-seasonal prediction. The ML models are compared against the Bureau of Meteorology's physics-based seasonal prediction system, ACCESS-S2, for the 38-year period, and a more recent physics-based coupled model for the shorter hindcast period. Across the two evaluation periods, both ML models have surprisingly good skill for sub-seasonal timescales, given they were designed for forecasting on medium range timescales. In general, the ML models are as skilful as the physical model ensemble mean at shorter lead times and comparable to the physical model ensemble members at longer lead times.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Evaluation of ML atmosphere models for sub-seasonal prediction, relevant to accelerating air quality modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估ML大气模型用于次季节预测，与加速空气质量建模相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估ML大气模型用于次季节预测，与加速空气质量建模相关

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates GraphCast and FourCastNetV2 for sub-seasonal prediction, showing potential of ML surrogates for atmospheric modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文评估了GraphCast和FourCastNetV2两种机器学习大气模型在次季节预测中的性能，涵盖马登-朱利安振荡和南半球环状模的表示能力。通过38年与2.5年回测对比，揭示了ML模型在扩展时间尺度的预报能力。尽管聚焦天气而非空气质量，其方法对加速化学传输模型和空气质量模拟具有参考价值。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 机器学习加速空气质量与化学传输模型, especially work that connects or combines: air quality model; CMAQ model air quality simulation; chemical transport model air pollution; physics-informed machine learning for environmental modeling; neural operator surrogate model; surrogate model air quality; deep learning methods for PM2.5 ozone air quality forecasting bias correction and data assimilation; CMAQ WRF CMAQ model acceleration GPU air quality simulation; physics informed neural operators for atmospheric transport advection diffusion and mass conservation; machine learning surrogate models for chemical transport and air quality models.

quick_skim.title: Sparse Sensor Placement for Reducing Forecast Errors in Ensemble Kalman Filtering
quick_skim.abstract: Designing efficient observation networks for reducing forecast errors is a fundamental challenge in numerical weather prediction. Data-driven sparse sensor placement (SSP) and ensemble-based data assimilation via the Ensemble Kalman Filter (EnKF) have each addressed this challenge independently, yet their mathematical connections have not been systematically formalized. This study presents a unified theoretical framework integrating SSP and EnKF through optimal experimental design, providing new theoretical and algorithmic results. While conventional SSP methods aim to reduce analysis errors, this study extends the SSP to target forecast error reduction by using a tangent linear model approximated by an ensemble forecast. We derive the Fisher information matrices in the ensemble and model spaces for the EnKF, and clarify the mathematical interpretations of A-, D-, and E-optimality in terms of forecast error reduction. A-optimality in the model space minimizes the mean forecast error variance; D-optimality is ill-defined in the model space due to rank deficiency and is therefore formulated in the ensemble space, where it maximizes the Shannon information content of assimilated observations; and E-optimality in the model space minimizes the worst-case forecast error variance. We further propose a fast greedy algorithm for selecting observation locations under A-optimality in the model space, avoiding matrix inversion at each greedy step and substantially reducing computational cost. Numerical experiments using the Lorenz-96 model support these theoretical findings. Among the three optimality criteria, A-optimality in the model space most consistently reduces forecast spread and root-mean-square error, and yields stable incremental improvements consistent with post-assimilation observation impact diagnostics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: sparse sensor placement for reducing forecast errors in ensemble kalman filtering
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于降低集成卡尔曼滤波预报误差的稀疏传感器布局

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Unifies sparse sensor placement and ensemble Kalman filtering through optimal experimental design to reduce forecast errors, relevant to observation network design for data assimilation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 将稀疏传感器布局与集成卡尔曼滤波统一到最优实验设计框架，以减少预报误差为目标。理论推导了Fisher信息矩阵与误差的关系，为空气质量预报中的观测网络设计提供理论依据。

quick_skim.title: NIVA: A Multimodal Foundation Model for Actionable Earth System Intelligence
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in AI-driven weather and climate modeling have improved forecast skill while reducing computational cost. However, existing data-driven approaches are limited in their ability to model coupled Earth system dynamics, which is required for extending predictability beyond the ~2-week horizon. To address this, we introduce NIVA, a multimodal foundation model designed to learn unified representations across Earth system components. While the full framework targets atmosphere, ocean, ice, and land interactions, we focus here on a two-modality setting (ocean and atmosphere) as a controlled proof of concept to evaluate whether foundation models can learn coupled dynamics. Trained on large-scale Earth system simulations, NIVA learns physically meaningful cross-modal structure, providing a foundation for subseasonal-to-seasonal prediction. As initial validation, we show that NIVA captures key modes of climate variability through accurate prediction of major climate indices.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal foundation model for Earth system intelligence including atmosphere and ocean modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于地球系统智能的多模态基础模型，包含大气和海洋建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于地球系统智能的多模态基础模型，包含大气和海洋建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于地球系统智能的多模态基础模型，包含大气和海洋建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces NIVA, a multimodal foundation model for coupled Earth system dynamics, trained on large-scale simulations, showing promise for physically consistent predictions of atmosphere and ocean; relevant to environmental modeling and air quality prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有数据驱动地球系统模型难以捕捉耦合动力学。本文提出NIVA多模态基础模型，学习大气、海洋等地球系统组件的统一表示，在大规模模拟数据上训练，展现出物理一致的预测能力。两模态（海洋-大气）概念验证表明其可扩展至更复杂环境，为空气质量模拟等提供新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有数据驱动地球系统模型难以捕捉耦合动力学。本文提出NIVA多模态基础模型，学习大气、海洋等地球系统组件的统一表示，在大规模模拟数据上训练，展现出物理一致的预测能力。两模态（海洋-大气）概念验证表明其可扩展至更复杂环境，为空气质量模拟等提供新工具。

quick_skim.title: A Trainable-by-Parts Operator Learning Framework: Bridging DeepONet and Karhunen-Loeve Expansions for Large-Scale Applications
quick_skim.abstract: Training operator-learning models for large-scale problems governed by partial differential equations (PDEs) is challenging due to the curse of dimensionality, memory constraints, and limited training data. These challenges arise in many scientific and engineering applications, including subsurface flow, climate modeling, and geological carbon storage (GCS). In this work, we propose a scalable operator-learning framework based on the Karhunen-Loeve Deep Neural Network (KL-DNN) and demonstrate its performance for modeling GCS. The model is trained on a dataset comprising 100 samples of large-scale simulations in a three-dimensional domain with 1.7 million cells and 50 time steps. The KL-DNN method constructs latent spaces using low-rank singular value decomposition of static properties and a nested Karhunen-Loeve expansion for dynamic pressure fields, enabling full-resolution predictions without subsampling or spatial coarsening. The KL-DNN model achieves an average root mean square error (RMSE) of 1.1 psi for pressure (0.04% relative error with respect to the average pressure in the domain) and RMSE of 0.0146 for CO2 saturation (5% relative error with respect to the average saturation inside the plume). The model requires 20 minutes of training on a single GPU, representing a 19% reduction in the pressure errors, 7% reduction in the saturation error, and a two-order-of-magnitude speedup compared to DeepONet trained on the same dataset. These results, along with inference time of less than one minute, establish the proposed model as a practical and accurate solution for large-scale PDE problems, enabling rapid uncertainty quantification, history matching, and real-time decision support.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: scalable operator learning framework (KL-DNN) for large-scale PDEs, applicable to climate modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可扩展算子学习框架KL-DNN，适用于气候建模等大规模PDE
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可扩展算子学习框架KL-DNN，适用于气候建模等大规模PDE
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可扩展算子学习框架KL-DNN，适用于气候建模等大规模PDE
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a scalable operator learning framework (KL-DNN) combining DeepONet and KL expansions for large-scale PDEs, demonstrated on geological carbon storage.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大规模偏微分方程算子学习中维数灾难和内存限制的难题，提出了一种结合Karhunen-Loeve展开与深度神经网络的框架（KL-DNN）。该方法通过可训练的分部策略实现高效学习，并在三维地质碳封存模拟（170万网格、50时间步）上验证了其准确性和可扩展性。该框架为加速气候建模等大规模环境模拟提供了可行方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大规模偏微分方程算子学习中维数灾难和内存限制的难题，提出了一种结合Karhunen-Loeve展开与深度神经网络的框架（KL-DNN）。该方法通过可训练的分部策略实现高效学习，并在三维地质碳封存模拟（170万网格、50时间步）上验证了其准确性和可扩展性。该框架为加速气候建模等大规模环境模拟提供了可行方案。
